game_name: Rocko's Modern Life: Spunky's Dangerous Day

danger_signals.meaning: Physical threats requiring avoidance or navigation skill
Active enemies that pursue or block progression
Unpredictable or dynamic hazards that change state
Automated or structural dangers in modern suburban setting

safety_signals.visual_form: Spunky character sprite with idle/walking animation
safety_signals.meaning: Player objective—keep this entity alive and moving forward

safety_signals.visual_form: Open yard/block space without active hazards
safety_signals.meaning: Safe ground for movement and escort progression
Passive elements player can use or activate for progression

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Yard-based domestic setting with suburban obstacles
Must remain protected in foreground; tied to player success state

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Bee animation, machine sprites with movement patterns
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Scattered throughout yard; represent modern suburban dangers

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Spunky stationary or moving forward; obstacles visible but navigable
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Rocko controls movement; Spunky follows or remains in protected zone

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Animated hazard sprites (geyser eruption, bee flight, mower movement)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Rocko must dodge; if Spunky hit, likely failure state indicated

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Button sprite becomes active (visual state change likely)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mechanical response—gate opens, pillar moves, or hazard deactivates

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Hazard animation intensifies (steam, splash, pillar creaking sound/visual)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Rocko must react quickly; Spunky vulnerability increases

environmental_storytelling.example: Yard transformed from domestic space to obstacle course—lawn mower, garden geyser, yard bee all weaponized by narrative context (Spunky's dangerous day)
Players familiar with Rocko's Modern Life recognize visual props (geyser, mower) as recurring hazards from show; environmental design leverages existing IP knowledge
Gun sprite, steam, flush mechanics suggest household/garden tools become threats—visual metaphor for suburban mundanity turned perilous
Creak, thud, bounce sounds tied to sprite animations indicate object state changes and narrative progression through physical manifestation

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Remains in designated area; follows Rocko or waits stationary
Likely animation change (panic, fleeing) or loss condition triggered
Flight pattern animation; predictable movement arc
Accelerated animation; direct pursuit toward Rocko/Spunky
Movement animation; sweeping action; hazard zone around blade area
Explosive splash animation; temporary hazard zone; likely triggered by proximity or time
Visible but no effect; neutral sprite state
Activates linked hazard deactivation or path opening; visual feedback (depression/highlight)
Unused animation suggests planned directional behavior (turning toward camera for emphasis or mode change)

notes: This is an escort platformer based on Nickelodeon IP. The manifestation layer is primarily visual—hazard sprites, animation states, and environmental props communicate danger. The world-state is largely binary (safe navigation vs. active hazard) with mechanical state-changes (buttons, geyser triggers). Faction identity is implicit through character role (player, escort, enemy) rather than explicit visual markers like color or sigil. The suburban yard setting serves dual purpose: familiar IP context and threat landscape. Available source data is limited to development notes and unused graphics; comprehensive behavioral observation requires gameplay footage analysis.
